title: CRM Sync Without Copy-Paste for Web Design Agencies
metaDescription: Web design agencies: keep your CRM in sync with live data from forms, inboxes, and proposals. No copy-paste, no stale records, no missed follow-ups.
excerpt: A done-for-you Connections layer that keeps your CRM updated from live data across forms, inboxes, calendars, and proposals, so no one copies and pastes leads.

sections.intro:
Web design agencies leak information between tools. A lead fills out a contact form, a project manager logs the call in a doc, the designer drops notes in a shared file, and the CRM still says the lead is 'new' three weeks later. By the time anyone reports on pipeline, the data is stale and someone is rebuilding the picture from memory.

The Ayothedoc AI Operating System fixes this at the Connections layer. We wire your CRM to the places where real work already happens, the inbox, the calendar, the proposal tool, the form on your site, so records update themselves from live data. No one copies and pastes a lead again. The owner still owns the system, the rules are visible, and the CRM finally reflects what is actually happening.
sections.fourCFit:
This page sits in the Connections layer of the AIOS. Connections is the plumbing that lets the rest of the system work from live data instead of guesses.

  • Context: the system already knows your services, your pricing tiers, and your sales stages.
  • Connections: this page. Your CRM is kept in sync with forms, email, calendar, and your proposal or invoicing tool.
  • Capabilities: drafting follow-ups, summarizing discovery calls, and routing handoffs depend on a CRM that is actually current.
  • Cadence: nightly and hourly checks reconcile records so nothing drifts.

If you have not seen the entry point yet, the free 60-Second Lead Engine sits on top of this same Connections layer.
sections.whatDoneLooksLike:
You get a working sync, not a diagram. Concretely:

  • Every new lead from your website form, inbox, or referral channel creates or updates a CRM contact within 60 seconds.
  • Discovery calls booked on your calendar attach to the matching deal automatically, with the meeting link, attendees, and stage.
  • Proposals sent and signed move the deal stage without anyone touching the CRM.
  • Project kickoff fields (scope, tier, start date) flow from the signed proposal into the CRM and your project tracker.
  • A weekly reconciliation report flags any record that looks stale, duplicated, or stuck so you can clean it in one pass.
  • SLA: lead-to-CRM under 60 seconds, stage changes within 5 minutes of the trigger, weekly drift report every Monday.
sections.howWeInstall:
1. Audit (free, about 10 minutes). We map every place a lead or project record currently lives, where it gets out of date, and which fields actually matter. Run the audit.
  2. Install (10 business days). We connect your CRM to your form, inbox, calendar, and proposal tool. We define the field map, the stage rules, and the reconciliation checks. You approve the rules in writing before anything goes live.
  3. Operate (ongoing). The sync runs on its own. We add one new automation per week, monitor the drift report, and adjust as your services and stages change. See plans.
sections.expectedResults:
• Time spent updating CRM records drops by 4 to 8 hours per week for a 5 to 15 person agency.
  • Pipeline reports become trustworthy again, usually within the first two weeks.
  • Lead response time falls from hours or days to under a minute, because the CRM no longer waits on a human to log the lead.
  • Duplicate contacts and orphaned deals drop sharply once weekly reconciliation is running.
  • Standing guarantee: across your AIOS, you recover 40 or more hours a month or we keep working free until you do.
sections.faq:
Do we have to switch CRMs?

No. We are tool-agnostic and work with the CRM you already pay for. If your current CRM genuinely cannot support the sync, we will say so during the audit and explain the trade-offs.

What happens to the records we already have?

We leave historical data in place. The install sets the rules going forward and produces a one-time cleanup list for the records that are obviously broken, so you decide what to fix.

Who owns the automations and the data?

You do. Every connection, rule, and field map lives in your accounts under your logins. If you ever stop working with us, the system keeps running and you keep the documentation.
